rulings.Case Title: MARK GENKIN VS. LEONID TRUBNIKOV ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Reclassify This Case To Unlimited Jurisdiction
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, February 21, 2017 line 3. PLAINTIFF MARK GENKIN's Motion To Reclassify This Case To Unlimited Jurisdiction DENIED. Plaintiff's motion is filed in violation of CCP 1005 and without leave of Court. Additionally, Plaintiff failed to sustain its burden and show with competent admissible evidence that the case is incorrectly classified and good cause for not bringing this motion earlier. It is impossible to determine based on counsel declaration how and why the damages reached jurisdictional limit in early February 2017 based on Ilia Genkin's November 2016 declaration that "In August 2016, using Zillow.com, I determined the market rent to be $4,500." At this rate jurisdictional limit would have been reached in June 2016. No other specific basis for counsel's determination provided.= (501/NJL)
rulings.Judge: Judge Pro Tem: Noah J. Lebowitz

rulings.Case Title: CHARLES MA VS. CATHERINE HERRERA ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, February 21, 2017 line 4. DEFENDANT CATHERINE HERRERA MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT Moot.= (501/NJL)
rulings.Judge: Judge Pro Tem: Noah J. Lebowitz

rulings.Case Title: CHARLES MA VS. CATHERINE HERRERA ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, February 21, 2017 line 4. DEFENDANT CATHERINE HERRERA DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT Sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege in good faith and explain the change in factual allegations regarding the basis of the existence of landlord-tenant relationship. If Plaintiff's verified amended complaint sufficiently alleges oral rental agreement between Plaintiff (or predecessor in interest, etc.), Plaintiff does not need to attach any agreements if they do not form the basis for this action.= (501/NJL)
rulings.Judge: Judge Pro Tem: Noah J. Lebowitz
